latency_methodology: Both tools measured in-process as libraries (no HTTP), on the same 10 frozen files in bench/corpus_headroom/*.json, same machine (Apple M-series, macOS 15.7.2 arm64). Per file: 1 untimed warmup call + 5 timed calls, wall-clock time.perf_counter() (Python) / time.Now()-time.Since() (Go), median of the 5 reported. headroom-ai==0.30.0, headroom.compress(messages, model='claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929'), default CompressConfig, same message-wrapping rule as the reduction benchmark above (3 conversation-shaped files passed as message arrays: agentic_conversation_anthropic.json, agentic_conversation_openai.json, rag_conversation.json; the other 7 plain-JSON data files wrapped as messages=[{'role':'tool','content':<raw file bytes>}]). whittle v0.2.1 (git describe: v0.2.1-4-gb5b419f), one whittle.Engine built via whittle.New(whittle.Options{ModelURL: "", MinTokens: 0}) reused across all files and calls, eng.Compress(ctx, string(rawFileBytes)) timed directly (no HTTP daemon, no JSON request/response marshaling). Both measurements exclude process startup, model/venv import, and file I/O -- only the compress call itself is timed. A second full pass of both benchmarks showed run-to-run variance (headroom mostly +/-0.2ms; whittle up to ~1ms on the two smallest conversation-shaped files, including one GC-driven outlier run of ~6.6ms that was NOT the run selected as median) -- the numbers below are from one designated run per tool (1 warmup + 5 timed), not cherry-picked across repeated passes.
